title: Device & Media Controls — USB Mass Storage on ePHI Workstations

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
spl:
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration" EventCode="400" earliest=-7d
| where match(ClassName,"USBSTOR|Disk&Ven_")
| lookup clinical_workstations.csv ComputerName OUTPUT contains_ephi_workstation
| where contains_ephi_workstation="true"
| stats count by ComputerName, ClassName, DeviceID
| sort - count
value: Captures removable media mount attempts on endpoints that access Hyperspace so device and media control policies for ePHI workstations are enforceable and auditable.
implementation: (1) Enforce BitLocker + DLP on exceptions; (2) require temporary USB grants via ServiceNow with ticket ID in log annotation optional; (3) quarterly exception review.
visualization: Table (host, device class, count), Single value (violations).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
